description_long: This is a vintage-style biplane painted primarily in blue with white and yellow accents, featuring a wooden two-blade propeller at the nose. The aircraft has a single open cockpit situated just behind the upper wing, with a visible windscreen and a white eagle insignia on the fuselage. The wings are straight and slightly dihedral, with roundels on the upper surfaces and struts connecting the upper and lower wings. The landing gear is fixed and consists of two main wheels with blue and white covers and a small tail skid. The tail section includes a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizer, both painted blue with yellow trim on the rudder. The overall design suggests a lightweight, maneuverable aircraft typical of early 20th-century aviation.
description: A blue vintage biplane with a wooden propeller, open cockpit, and fixed landing gear, featuring roundels and a white eagle insignia.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wooden propeller, round engine cowling, and the symmetrical biplane wings with struts.
description_view_1: Oblique top-side view highlighting the open cockpit, upper wing roundels, and the eagle insignia on the fuselage.
description_view_2: Top-down view displaying the full wingspan, dihedral angle, and the tail's horizontal and vertical stabilizers.
description_view_3: Oblique underside view showing the fixed landing gear, tail skid, and the lower wing structure.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane with straight, slightly dihedral upper and lower wings; no winglets or swept features; strut-braced.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a two-blade wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing with a small windscreen.
